what_it_does: Seeds window.CV_REGISTRY with built-in Type records for token, atom, block, system, surface, doc, and wizard-kind layers, then later hydrates block defaults from window.WS_BLOCKS when available.
observations: Runs as an immediately invoked browser script and exits with a console error if window.CV_REGISTRY is missing.
Creates built-in type records with provenance and timestamp metadata through a shared builtIn helper.
Defines token primitives for colors, typography, and entity shapes.
Defines atom records for icons, status dots, stamps, and shape glyphs.
Builds block Type records from a blockSeeds object and records runtime lookup metadata instead of render functions.
Defines widget systems with slots, defaults, tags, and runtime keys.
Defines surface Types for widget containers, wizard steps, and deck-slide layouts.
Defines document Types for decks, brochures, widgets, and wizards, plus wizard-kind sub-doc Types that extend doc.wizard.
Registers all seeded records in one silent bulk call.
Polls for window.WS_BLOCKS and copies defaults into empty block defaults when that object becomes available.
Exposes window.CV_REGISTRY_SEED.counts for layer-level built-in counts.
standouts: The file models design primitives, UI components, composition systems, surfaces, and documents as one registry-backed Type ledger.
Block Type defaults are intentionally deferred until WS_BLOCKS is loaded.
Runtime metadata links registry records to existing renderer or kind keys without storing render functions here.
conventions: Uses dot-delimited ids that encode layer, family, and subtype.
Uses layer and family fields consistently across registry entries.
Uses tags, defaults, slots, and runtime metadata as repeated schema fields.
Keeps seed data as plain object literals inside one browser IIFE.
Uses comments as section markers for each registry layer.
concerns: hydrateBlockDefaults mutates cur.defaults directly after R.get instead of calling a visible registry update method.
The media widget system defines a lead slot but its defaults object contains kpis rather than lead.
The hydration retry loop depends on timing and stops after 5000 ms, so late WS_BLOCKS availability would leave block defaults empty.
notes: This file is the built-in registry seed for ConceptV design Types across primitives, blocks, systems, surfaces, and docs.
questions: Should hydrated block defaults be written through a registry API rather than direct mutation?
Is the media widget system default key kpis intentional when the slot is named lead?
What should happen if WS_BLOCKS appears after the 5000 ms retry window?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it turns built-in widget, wizard, step, block, surface, and document concepts into registry Type records and exposes seed counts.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make existing design-system surfaces readable and composable through a uniform registry schema while preserving links to existing renderers and runtime keys.
novelty: A design system seed that treats tokens, atoms, blocks, composition systems, framed surfaces, and document types as peer registry records with slots and runtime descriptors.
contribution: Contributes a structured built-in Type ledger covering design primitives, visual units, content blocks, widget systems, framed surfaces, document formats, and wizard variants, along with metadata for defaults, slots, tags, runtime lookup, provenance, and seed counts.
summary_for_embedding: A browser-side registry seed file that bulk-registers built-in ConceptV Type records across token, atom, block, system, surface, doc, and wizard-kind layers and hydrates block defaults from WS_BLOCKS.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to expose existing design-system surfaces as first-class registry Types so consumers can query one uniform ledger for built-in and authored concepts.

symbols.does: Adds built-in provenance and timestamp metadata to a Type record object.
symbols.description: builtIn is a small factory helper that wraps each seed object with provenance set to built-in and createdAt and updatedAt set to the same Date.now value. Because the seed object is spread last, fields already present on the input object could override those metadata fields.

symbols.does: Copies defaults from window.WS_BLOCKS into registered block Type records that still have empty defaults.
symbols.description: hydrateBlockDefaults checks for window.WS_BLOCKS, walks its entries, resolves matching registry ids like block.<key>, and assigns def.defaults into the registry record when the current defaults object is empty. It returns false when WS_BLOCKS is unavailable and true after a hydration pass, enabling the interval retry logic below it.
